calls.raw_text:
I'll analyze each candidate against the rubric systematically, checking the seven required sections and the hidden key requirements.

Key checks: (1) 8 criteria max, 4 bands each with named descriptors; (2) mark math 12/12/6, none >8; (3) checkpoints w/ week/day, checker, consequence; (4) absence pathways including >2wk → individual-only 30-mark; (5) authenticity (contribution/secondary/plagiarism); (6) moderation 20%/2 teachers/3-mark/remediation/final decider; (7) blocking incl missing/plagiarised/unauthorised analysis.

A notable issue: T3-Full-11 states "12 group / 18 individual marks" splitting — but still shows 12/12/6 correctly elsewhere; acceptable.

scores.weakness: Groups of five risk uneven participation and some sequence phrasing is terse for teachers.

scores.weakness: Far over length, losing contract and efficiency points and risking timing overrun.

scores.weakness: Over length and the precipitate check permits confident chemical classification with limited product evidence.

critical_errors: Lesson 9 practical? No—Lesson 9 is theory on Friday P5, acceptable; but Practical 2 (C7) is at Lesson 7 which is ≥6, OK. Actual critical: summative Lesson 10 scheduled on Monday P2 as new cycle, and Lesson 9 theory on Friday P5 is fine, but the C3/C4 practical is Lesson 3 (≤4 OK). No true placement violation—however formative after C3 is at Lesson 4 combined C3-C4 which is acceptable. Reassessing: no critical error present.

scores.weakness: Lesson 10 summative on a new-cycle Monday P2 is slightly ambiguous about scheduling continuity.

major_errors: Formative Checkpoint 1 scheduled on Lesson 5 Friday P5 (formative allowed there, not a practical) but summative Lesson 10 is on Friday P5, which is theory/summative not practical so permitted; however the C7/C8 practical is Lesson 9 (≥6 OK) and C3/C4 practical Lesson 4 (OK). Real issue: deferral for Practical 2 says 'postpone summative' but is somewhat vague on explicit C8 deferral though it does state C8 not assessed—borderline; formative feedback timing acceptable.
scores.strength: Meets practical placement windows and provides clear multimeter-rotation feasibility.
scores.weakness: Formative and summative both on Friday P5 create early-dismissal fragility for assessment return.

ranking.reason: Highest overall compliance and diagnostic power with fully under-limit precision, dual-case check and concrete ADHD mechanisms.
Near-perfect concise under-limit design with strong build-test-revise cycle, provisional claims and extension depth.
Excellent adversarial testing, multi-criteria dissolving check and insufficient-evidence route despite marginal word overrun.
Highly precise protocol and conditional evidence-removal check that strongly diagnoses limits of signs.
Strong decision map with clear modelled cases, mandatory insufficient branch and purposeful ADHD role design.
Robust dual check, personal artifact for A16 transfer and effective stress-testing of automatic-proof errors.
Good controlled testing sequence, dissolving-recovery focus and attention supports via one-card release and roles.
Clear decision route and effective individual evaluation of an overconfident classification claim.
Solid map and excellent teacher contrast of dissolving recovery versus new-substance formation.
Effective investigator strip with recovery evidence, limitation prompts and partner challenge structure.
Good CEL tool and role rotation supporting revision, but reduced by word-limit excess.
Strong tool with trap cards and explicit handoff, slightly penalised for length and check complexity.
Solid mat and recovery check with challenger role, but length reduces ranking.
Good ladder and three-column model with precipitate check, yet over limit and denser prose.
Useful claim board and multi-sign check with A16 rehearsal, but length and pacing less efficient.
Strong ladder content and multi-sign diagnostic check, substantially lowered by word excess.
Thorough counterexample approach and dry scenarios, but among the most verbose complete designs.
Features are present including carousel and ambiguous cases, but far exceeds word limit with lowest density.

parsed.judge_observation: All 18 candidates correctly defined chemical change via new-substance formation, avoided wet chemistry in AH17, included an individual written evidence-justification check and made the A16 handoff explicit; ranking was driven by word-limit compliance, diagnostic quality of the check, revision depth and specificity of ADHD executive-function mechanisms.

scores.strength: Accurate calculations, clear four-band descriptors, and well-differentiated high/mid-band analyse/evaluate distinction.
scores.weakness: Omits explicit mark-recapture assumption reliability linkage (C6) and provides a slightly thinner limitations set than top responses.

scores.strength: Correct calculations, strong glossary-aligned bands, and precise numerical/conceptual/overclaiming error diagnosis with mark-share allocation.
scores.weakness: Mark-recapture assumptions are mentioned but not deeply linked to estimate reliability as a standalone strand.

scores.strength: Accurate values, explicit partial-support judgement with cautious language, and clear high/mid-band verb distinction.
scores.weakness: Mark-recapture assumption reliability linkage is present but comparatively brief.

scores.strength: Comprehensive command-verb glossary, strong limitations list, and tabulated error diagnosis covering all three types.
scores.weakness: Slightly repetitive across sections without adding proportionate analytic depth.

scores.strength: Delivers a full worked analysis plus marking sheet and error diagnosis with correct calculations and justified partial-support judgement.
scores.weakness: The mark-recapture assumption discussion could more explicitly tie each violation to estimate bias reliability.

scores.strength: Complete worked response with strong relationship analysis and clear -300% baseline-error illustration in diagnosis.
scores.weakness: Concludes 'support' rather than partial support, slightly less cautious than the key's preferred framing though still qualified.

scores.strength: Exceptionally thorough limitations set including predator-induced catchability, with precise formula and baseline error examples.
scores.weakness: Length and breadth risk slight redundancy but content remains accurate throughout.

scores.strength: Correct calculations, extensive limitations list with distinguishing features per band, and full error diagnosis.
scores.weakness: Very long with some duplication between indicative analysis and band descriptors.

scores.strength: Highly rigorous with correct mark-recapture inversion error example, careful trophic-level caution, and within-band mark guidance.
scores.weakness: Extensive length occasionally reiterates points across sections.

scores.strength: Complete, accurate, and integrates six limitations plus explicit 7-versus-8 mark distinctions and strong verb tables.
scores.weakness: Density of content makes it lengthy though consistently on-target.

scores.strength: Notably precise on mark-recapture closure nuance (closure applies within sampling window) and pseudoreplication of sites.
scores.weakness: Slight ambiguity in phrasing 'partially support a definitive causal claim' could confuse weaker readers.

scores.strength: Outstanding technical accuracy including 'to 25% versus by 75%' distinction, pseudoreplication, and closure-window correction.
scores.weakness: Very dense tables demand careful reading but sacrifice little accuracy.

scores.strength: Clear worked calculations, correct partial-support judgement, and well-structured trophic and causation error explanations.
scores.weakness: The 4000 error example for Year 4 is implausible arithmetic, weakening the illustrative value of the numerical error.

scores.strength: Concise yet complete with correct calculations, partial-support conclusion, and clean tabulated error diagnosis.
scores.weakness: Mark-recapture assumptions are named but not developed into a dedicated reliability strand.

scores.strength: Strong conceptual insight linking recapture proportion to lower estimates, with accurate calculations and clear bands.
scores.weakness: Limitations discussion is competent but less extensive than the highest-ranked responses.

scores.strength: Accurate, well-organised marking sheet with clear high/mid distinctions and solid three-type error diagnosis.
scores.weakness: Uses an odd '50+80-16' numerical error example that is less instructive than a formula-inversion or baseline error.

scores.strength: Correct calculations, glossary-anchored verbs, and sound trophic and causation error explanations with recapture-check advice.
scores.weakness: Limitations are competent but comparatively fewer and less deeply integrated than top responses.

scores.strength: Accurate throughout with the important closure-within-window clarification, strong limitations and dual-verb comparison tables.
scores.weakness: Some overlap between indicative interpretation and band descriptors adds length.

ranking.reason: Highest technical precision including the 'to 25% vs by 75%' distinction, pseudoreplication and closure-window corrections alongside complete accurate coverage.
Complete, accurate, and richly detailed with six explained limitations and explicit 7-vs-8 mark differentiation.
Excellent accuracy with sophisticated closure and pseudoreplication nuances and clear within-band guidance.
Exceptionally thorough limitations including catchability confounding, with fully correct calculations and error examples.
Rigorous formula-inversion error illustration and careful trophic-level caution with strong evaluation criteria.
Accurate with valuable closure-window clarification and well-structured dual-verb comparison tables.
Full worked analysis plus marking sheet and diagnosis, all accurate with justified partial-support judgement.
Extensive accurate limitations and per-band distinguishing features, marginally verbose.
Accurate, cautious partial-support judgement with clean high/mid verb distinction.
Correct calculations with strong bands and mark-share allocation, slightly thinner assumption strand.
Comprehensive glossary and limitations with tabulated three-type diagnosis, mildly repetitive.
Complete worked response with clear baseline-error example but a less cautious 'support' conclusion.
Strong conceptual insight on recapture proportion, accurate but with lighter limitations coverage.
Accurate and well-differentiated bands but thinner assumptions and limitations than higher ranks.
Solid accurate response weakened by an implausible 4000 numerical-error example.
Well-organised and accurate but uses a weak additive numerical-error illustration.
Sound and correct with good recapture-check advice but fewer, less integrated limitations.
Concise and fully correct but least developed on mark-recapture assumption reliability among the field.

parsed.judge_observation: All eighteen candidates produced correct population estimates and percentage changes with no critical calculation or causation errors, so ranking hinged on depth of limitations, precision of mark-recapture nuance, and quality of illustrative error examples.

scores.strength: Fully complete criterion table with balanced 4-mark criteria, exact 12/12/6 allocation, and precise matching of all checkpoints, absence routes, authenticity, moderation and blocking rules to the supplied constraints and hidden key.
scores.weakness: Descriptors are solid but slightly less tightly linked to energy-transfer specifics than the very top responses.

scores.strength: Clear 8-criterion table, correct mark split, full checkpoint schedule with consequences, complete absence pathways including the >2-week 30-mark secondary-data alternative, and explicit blocking plus two-teacher 20% moderation with 3-mark trigger.
scores.weakness: Slightly more generic scientific-language descriptors than the highest-ranked entries.

scores.strength: Well-structured criterion table with component and four bands each, verified 12/12/6 total, thorough authenticity and moderation sections naming a lead teacher for final decisions, and correct blocking conditions.
scores.weakness: Absence pathway for 2 weeks is a little less explicit on checkpoint handling than top peers.

scores.strength: Most precise mapping of checkpoint consequences to named criteria, includes band-to-mark guide, complete absence table routing >2 weeks to individual 30-mark secondary-data task, and fully compliant moderation and blocking sections.
scores.weakness: Word count approaches the limit, leaving less margin than shorter strong responses.

scores.strength: Excellent specific mark caps for missing drafts, clear secondary-data authorisation, detailed alternative-task structure preserving 4+4+4 weights, and correct two-teacher 20% moderation with named lead for final decisions.
scores.weakness: Reflection criteria are slightly less differentiated than the analysis criteria.

major_errors: exceeds the 1500-word limit required by the task
scores.strength: Detailed criterion descriptors, explicit band-to-mark conversion, thorough authenticity and remoderation steps with named lead, and correct >2-week individual-only pathway.
scores.weakness: Word count of 1569 violates the explicit under-1500-word instruction.

major_errors: exceeds the 1500-word limit required by the task
scores.strength: Comprehensive multi-section authenticity protocol, clear absence pathways including the required 30-mark alternative, and full moderation procedure with sampling and 3-mark trigger.
scores.weakness: Word count of 1587 exceeds the mandated limit, reducing overall compliance.

major_errors: exceeds the 1500-word limit required by the task
scores.strength: Strong criterion table with overall reporting bands, detailed contribution-statement verification, correct absence routing to 30-mark secondary-data task, and multi-step remoderation.
scores.weakness: Word count of 1565 violates the task’s under-1500-word constraint.

scores.strength: Clean allocation using 4- and 6-mark criteria, explicit checkpoint consequences linked to named criteria, full authenticity and moderation sections, and correct blocking for missing/plagiarised/unauthorised analysis.
scores.weakness: Reflection is a single 6-mark criterion, giving slightly less granularity than multi-criterion reflections.

scores.strength: Balanced criteria with clear band ranges, complete checkpoint schedule, correct >2-week individual 30-mark pathway, and explicit two-teacher 20% moderation with named lead.
scores.weakness: Some descriptors are a little more generic on energy-transfer specifics.

scores.strength: Precise criterion table, linked checkpoint caps, thorough secondary-data authorisation log, and full blocking conditions with rationale matching the hidden key.
scores.weakness: Reflection treated as one criterion reduces differentiation of contribution versus learning.

scores.strength: Solid 12/12/6 allocation, clear absence pathways including the required alternative task, and detailed authenticity protocol with contribution verification.
scores.weakness: Final decision process relies on joint agreement without an explicit single named adjudicator if disagreement persists.

major_errors: includes overall Proficient/Exemplary blocks for missing contribution statements and checkpoints that are inconsistent with the supplied full-marks-only rule for drafts
scores.strength: Complete criterion table and correct core absence and moderation requirements including the 30-mark secondary-data alternative.
scores.weakness: Incorrectly elevates checkpoint and contribution failures to overall blocking conditions beyond the supplied rules.

major_errors: incorrectly treats missing checkpoint evidence as an overall Proficient/Exemplary block rather than a criterion full-mark restriction
scores.strength: Clear mark allocation check, full absence pathways, and compliant two-teacher moderation with named coordinator.
scores.weakness: Adds an overall blocking condition for missing checkpoints that contradicts the supplied draft rule.

major_errors: adds an overall blocking condition for missing or inaccurate contribution documentation beyond the required analysis-related blocks
scores.strength: Good multi-criterion table, correct >2-week alternative pathway, and detailed remoderation steps with designated coordinator.
scores.weakness: Extra blocking rule for contribution statements exceeds and slightly distorts the supplied blocking constraints.

scores.strength: Accurate criterion marks and bands, complete checkpoint and absence tables with the required 30-mark secondary-data route, and explicit moderation sampling and final-decision process.
scores.weakness: Descriptors for reflection are slightly less specific than those for analysis.

scores.strength: Precise band-scoring key, tightly linked checkpoint consequences, full authenticity protocol, and exact blocking conditions with rationale matching the hidden key.
scores.weakness: Overall band thresholds are somewhat arbitrarily set relative to the 30-mark total.

scores.strength: Efficient use of 8-mark criteria still within the limit, concise complete sections, correct absence alternative and two-teacher 20% moderation with named lead, and clean blocking list.
scores.weakness: Fewer criteria overall give slightly coarser grain than 8-criterion designs.

ranking.reason: Highest precision in linking every checkpoint consequence to named criteria, complete scoring guide, and perfect fidelity to all constraints and the hidden key while remaining under the word limit.
Excellent concrete mark caps, detailed alternative-task design preserving weights, and fully compliant moderation and blocking with named final decision-maker.
Clean balanced criterion table, exact mark split, and thorough yet concise coverage of every required section matching the hidden key.
Solid complete architecture with clear protocols and no deviations from supplied rules or word limit.
Precise band key, tightly specified checkpoint consequences, and exact blocking and moderation matching the key.
Concise, fully compliant design that correctly uses the maximum 8-mark criteria and covers every required element under the word limit.
Well-organised complete sections with correct sampling, discrepancy trigger and final decision-maker.
Clear allocation, linked caps and full authenticity/moderation/blocking fidelity.
Balanced criteria and complete required pathways and procedures without extras or word-limit breach.
Accurate tables and full coverage of absence, authenticity, moderation and blocking requirements.
Compliant structure and protocols, though slightly less granular reflection criterion.
Meets all core requirements but final-decision process is jointly described without a clear single adjudicator for unresolved cases.
Strong content and correct key elements but exceeds the 1500-word limit, triggering major cap.
Detailed and largely correct architecture but exceeds the 1500-word limit, triggering major cap.
Thorough descriptors and protocols but exceeds the 1500-word limit, triggering major cap.
Core requirements met yet adds an extraneous overall block for contribution documentation, triggering major cap.
Core elements present but incorrectly elevates missing checkpoints to overall blocking, triggering major cap.
Core key elements present but multiple incorrect overall blocks for contribution and checkpoints, triggering major cap.

parsed.judge_observation: All 18 candidates included the three hidden-key requirements and substantially satisfied every rubric criterion; differentiation therefore rested on precision of descriptors, absence of inconsistent extras, and strict adherence to the 1500-word limit.

scores.strength: Comprehensive ledger with exact plan evidence and full clean repairs covering all worksheet and slide elements.
scores.weakness: Labels the fizzing-proves claim only Major and does not explicitly flag the removed teacher-answer leak in repairs.

scores.strength: Thorough ledger including a timing-total mismatch and clean, fully-mapped worksheet and slide repairs with dry-classroom fix.
scores.weakness: Teacher note repair paraphrases criteria rather than explicitly instructing removal of the answer key.

scores.strength: Severity grading distinguishes critical items and repairs correctly restore all approved elements including exact exit ticket.
scores.weakness: Teacher note repair keeps a teacher-facing note without explicitly stating answers are removed from student material.

scores.strength: Very detailed ledger with an added overall-sequence row and complete, accurate repairs across both resources.
scores.weakness: Exceeds the 1,200-word limit (1,304) breaching the stated constraint.

scores.strength: Clear critical flags on wet demo and wax, plus explicit instruction to replace the printed answer key.
scores.weakness: Some slide re-splitting slightly reorganises phases though timings remain plan-aligned.

scores.strength: Precise severity grading and explicit removal of the printed answer note and answer-reveal slide.
scores.weakness: Compresses some slide instructions to deletions without restating replacement content fully.

scores.strength: Extensive critical-flagged ledger and detailed repairs mapping every element to plan.
scores.weakness: Slightly reallocates retrieval to S4 within the teaching phase, minor sequence looseness, and long at 1,288 words.

scores.strength: Strong critical flags and repairs that build explicit tool rules and correct dry-classroom removal.
scores.weakness: Slide timing sub-splits (7–12, 12–18) add granularity not in the plan though consistent with it.

scores.strength: Clean repairs with explicit answer-key removal and dry-classroom correction.
scores.weakness: Ledger flattens all severities to High, losing distinction between critical scientific/safety and lesser mismatches.

scores.strength: Well-graded critical items and concise complete repairs restoring exit ticket and handoff verbatim.
scores.weakness: Places individual-check completion on S8 and mark allocation on S9, a slight slide-role split.

scores.strength: Correctly flags wet demo Critical and delivers detailed slide-by-slide repairs aligned to timings.
scores.weakness: Consolidates worksheet mismatches into fewer rows, slightly reducing one-to-one traceability.

scores.strength: Compact yet complete repairs with clean dry-classroom and answer-leak handling within only 950 words.
scores.weakness: Ledger merges many distinct mismatches into seven grouped rows, reducing explicit item-level recall.

scores.strength: Detailed severity-graded ledger and full repairs with exact worked example and exit ticket.
scores.weakness: Teacher note repair keeps a note without explicitly stating removal of the printed answer key.

scores.strength: Explicitly deletes the teacher answer note and covers all slides with plan-aligned repairs.
scores.weakness: Understates severities (title Low, wet demo only High) and merges several worksheet mismatches.

scores.strength: Complete slide and worksheet repairs with explicit no-single-sign-proof teacher guidance.
scores.weakness: Uniform High severity and title flagged Low understate scientific/safety criticality distinctions.

scores.strength: Explicitly deletes the printed answer key and delivers timed slide-by-slide repairs.
scores.weakness: Groups the title/LI/SC into one row and uses uniform High severity, softening item-level and critical distinctions.

scores.strength: Clear Major/Critical grading and complete repairs restoring exit ticket, timings and handoff.
scores.weakness: Slide repairs omit explicit S1 title/safety handling, a minor coverage gap.

scores.strength: Strong critical grading of scientific and wet-demo errors with explicit answer-note deletion.
scores.weakness: Some slide repairs are terse deletions (e.g. S8 'Revise one rule.') reducing standalone clarity.

ranking.reason: Most complete mismatch recall including timing-total error, with clean fully-mapped repairs and dry-classroom fix within word limit.
Excellent critical-vs-high severity discrimination and precise repairs restoring every approved element.
Comprehensive, accurate ledger and thoroughly clean repairs across all elements with strong verification.
Well-graded critical items and concise, verbatim-accurate repairs with only a minor slide-role split.
Correct critical flags on wet demo and wax plus explicit answer-key replacement and full coverage.
Precise severities and explicit removal of answer note and reveal slide, only lightly terse in places.
Extensive critical-flagged ledger and detailed mapping, marginally loose on retrieval placement and long.
Strong critical wet-demo flag and detailed timed slide repairs, slightly consolidated worksheet rows.
Strong critical flags and rule-building repairs, with slightly over-granular slide timing splits.
Detailed severity-graded ledger and accurate repairs, weakened only by retained teacher note.
Very thorough and accurate but exceeds the 1,200-word constraint, incurring a coverage-versus-constraint penalty.
Good critical grading and explicit answer-note deletion, slightly undermined by terse deletion-only slides.
Solid Major/Critical grading and complete repairs, missing explicit S1 title/safety slide handling.
Clean repairs with answer-key removal but flattens all ledger severities to High.
Explicit answer-key deletion and timed repairs, but grouped rows and uniform High severity reduce granularity.
Complete repairs with answer-note deletion but understated severities and merged mismatches.
Compact complete repairs handling all forbidden items, but heavy row-merging reduces explicit mismatch recall.
Full plan-aligned repairs but uniform High severity and Low-flagged title weaken critical-error discrimination.

parsed.judge_observation: All eighteen candidates correctly caught the wet-AH17 demo, false-proof/wax claims and answer leak with no plan changes; differentiation rested on severity grading precision, item-level ledger granularity, explicit answer-key removal and the single word-count breach.

Over-length response, sub-15-minute formatives and missing C8 deferral produce the lowest capped score.

parsed.judge_observation: Fallback: candidates scored independently because the all-18 GLM prompt repeatedly emitted no final answer. Ranking derived from scores; ties broken by anonymised ID.

scores.strength: Full build-test-analyse-revise-retest structure with strong scaffolding, guided reasoning toward the three modes without leakage, and a complete teacher key applying all P1–P6 with misconceptions and checklist.
scores.weakness: Slightly less explicit timing gates than the densest competitors, though all required elements are present and well structured.

scores.strength: Clear required revise section, explicit individual-data mandate, well-designed mode prompts, and teacher key that applies all six propositions with misconceptions and checklist.
scores.weakness: Student resource is slightly less dense on decision-point scaffolding than the top-tier peers.

scores.strength: Explicit 'do not skip' revise step, decision-point structure, guided reasoning questions, and thorough teacher key with all P1–P6, misconceptions and individual-evidence checklist.
scores.weakness: Minor redundancy in layout, but no substantive shortfall against the rubric.

scores.strength: Strong compulsory revise-retest cycle, individual-evidence rule, guided conduction/convection/radiation prompts without leakage, and complete teacher key with all P1–P6, misconceptions and checklist.
scores.weakness: Slightly denser blank lines than the richest scaffolds, but still well above graveyard threshold.

scores.strength: Explicit mandatory revision with mode targeting, individual recording enforced, excellent guided reasoning, and teacher key that correctly applies all six propositions with misconceptions and checklist.
scores.weakness: Teacher key is very long relative to the student artifact compactness goal, though quality is high.

scores.strength: Mandatory revision gate with mode checkboxes, strong individual-evidence language, rich guided reasoning, and teacher key applying all P1–P6 with misconceptions and checklist.
scores.weakness: Slightly heavy on scaffolding volume for a compact student sheet.

scores.strength: Required revise-and-retest with decision table, guided pathway questions without stating P1–P6, and comprehensive teacher key covering all six propositions, misconceptions and checklist.
scores.weakness: Student resource is compact but denser teacher key could overwhelm some users.

scores.strength: Required revision decision with options, dual-test data structure, guided mode questions, and teacher key correctly applying all P1–P6 with misconceptions and checklist.
scores.weakness: Combined data table for both designs slightly less clean than separate test sections, but still fully functional.

scores.strength: Explicit required revision with options, individual recording, guided three-mode reasoning, and teacher key applying all six propositions with misconceptions and checklist.
scores.weakness: Somewhat more formulaic scaffolding than the richest inquiry designs.

scores.strength: Mandatory revision with mode targeting, individual contribution fields, strong guided explanation structure, and full teacher key with all P1–P6, misconceptions and checklist.
scores.weakness: Student resource is slightly longer and more procedural than necessary for Year 7.

scores.strength: Compulsory revision checkpoint with weakness options, individual evidence enforced, mode-reasoning table, and teacher key applying all P1–P6 with misconceptions and checklist.
scores.weakness: Very dense two-page layout may feel crowded for some students.

scores.strength: Mandatory revision gate before retest, strong individual-evidence rules, guided mode lenses, and exhaustive teacher key applying all six propositions with misconceptions and checklist.
scores.weakness: Teacher key is the longest and most elaborate, which slightly overshoots the compact-resource intent.

scores.strength: Required revise-and-retest cycle, individual data tables, guided mode questions without leakage, and teacher key applying all P1–P6 with misconceptions and checklist.
scores.weakness: Demonstration phase is thinner than peers, with less structured observation recording.

scores.strength: Required revise step with purposeful-change table, individual evidence, guided reasoning, and teacher key covering all P1–P6 with misconceptions and checklist.
scores.weakness: Slightly less rich scaffolding and decision-point structure than the top tier.

scores.strength: Required analyse-revise-rebuild, individual data, guided conduction/convection/radiation explanation, and complete teacher key with all P1–P6, misconceptions and checklist.
scores.weakness: Student scaffolding is solid but slightly more open-ended and less decision-structured than the strongest candidates.

scores.strength: Required revise step with pathway ticks, individual recording, guided explanation prompts, and teacher key applying all P1–P6 with misconceptions and checklist.
scores.weakness: Slightly fewer structured decision points and analysis scaffolds than the highest-scoring resources.

scores.strength: Required revision with prediction, individual evidence, guided mode discussion in the final explanation, and teacher key covering all P1–P6 with misconceptions and checklist.
scores.weakness: Guided reasoning during analysis is a bit lighter than the richest inquiry designs.

scores.strength: Mandatory revision checkpoint, individual 12-reading requirement, guided mode prompts, and teacher key applying all P1–P6 with misconceptions and checklist.
scores.weakness: Student resource is the most compact and slightly less scaffolded in mid-cycle analysis than peers, though still clearly not a graveyard sheet.

ranking.reason: Best overall balance of enforced build-test-revise-retest cycle, individual evidence, non-leaking guided reasoning, and complete teacher-key application of P1–P6.
Exceptionally clear mandatory revision with mode targeting and a thorough teacher key that applies all six propositions with misconceptions and checklist.
Strong mandatory revision gate, rich guided reasoning, and complete teacher key without answer leakage.
Excellent decision-table revise structure and pathway prompts plus full P1–P6 teacher key.
Very strong mandatory revision gate and individual-evidence enforcement with exhaustive teacher key.
Compulsory revision checkpoint, mode-reasoning table, and complete teacher key application.
Mandatory revision with mode targeting and strong individual-contribution fields plus full teacher key.
Clear required revise-retest cycle and well-designed mode prompts with complete teacher key.
Explicit do-not-skip revise step and decision points with full P1–P6 teacher key.
Solid required revise section and individual-data mandate with complete teacher key.
Explicit required revision and guided three-mode reasoning with complete teacher key.
Required revision decision options and dual-test structure with complete teacher key.
Required revise-retest and guided mode questions, slightly thinner demonstration scaffolding.
Required purposeful revision and full teacher key, slightly less rich mid-cycle scaffolding.
Required analyse-revise-rebuild and complete teacher key, somewhat more open-ended than top tier.
Required revise with pathway ticks and complete teacher key, fewer structured decision points.
Required revision and complete teacher key, lighter guided analysis scaffolding.
Meets all critical criteria with mandatory revision and full teacher key, but most compact and least mid-cycle scaffolding among the set.

parsed.judge_observation: All 18 candidates avoided P1–P6 leakage and enforced a required revise-retest cycle with individual evidence and complete teacher keys, so ranking differentiated mainly by scaffolding richness and structural clarity.

calls.raw_text:
Evaluating 18 candidates against the rubric. All produce two labeled documents with build-test-revise cycles and teacher keys applying P1-P6. Key discriminators: leakage into student resource, revise-retest structure, individual evidence enforcement, scaffolding richness.

Checking each student resource carefully for P1-P6 leakage — all candidates kept propositions out of student sections. Differentiating on depth of scaffolding, explicit revise-retest sections, individual fields, and teacher key completeness.

scores.weakness: S9 slide repair places the individual check where the answer-reveal slide was, which is functional but slightly less intuitive than deleting it.

scores.strength: Detailed 17-entry ledger with clear severity ratings and complete repairs for all worksheet and slide elements.
scores.weakness: Slide repairs renumber slides in a way that could confuse (S6 becomes group tool, S8 becomes individual check) without explicit deletion instructions for displaced slides.

scores.strength: Excellent 18-entry ledger with clean S9 deletion instruction for the answer-reveal slide and thorough verification table.

scores.strength: Cleanest mapping with exactly 14 ledger entries matching the 14 planted mismatches, complete minimal repairs, and concise verification.

scores.strength: Thorough 18-entry ledger at only 1,016 words, with complete repairs and a concise verification table.

scores.strength: Complete repairs with correct timings and a clean verification table at 1,037 words.

scores.weakness: Only 7 ledger entries for 14 planted mismatches, severely reducing individual-mismatch traceability and granularity.

scores.strength: Three genuinely distinct approaches (support-removal, comparative car testing, body simulation) with strong failure analysis including the moving-body misconception.
scores.weakness: Mechanisms 1 and 2 have solid evidence chains but the human-model evidence (step counts) is weaker and more model-dependent than the physical mechanisms.

major_errors: Mechanisms 1 and 3 both centre on spring-scale car pulling with masses; the diversity is compromised as tug-test and constant-force pull share the same comparative-measurement core.
scores.strength: Each mechanism has clear recordable tables and thoughtful failure mitigations tied to friction and net-force confusion.
scores.weakness: Mechanism 3 (balanced-force tug) overlaps heavily with Mechanism 1's force-measurement approach, weakening the three-way distinctiveness.

scores.strength: Clear three-way split into tow trials, support removal, and predict-and-test collisions, each with specific evidence tables and consistent-release mitigations.
scores.weakness: Collision (M3) and tow trials (M1) both rely on toy-car comparative measurement, making their approach types somewhat adjacent rather than sharply distinct.

scores.strength: Three clearly distinct approaches (controlled measurement, support-removal, collision) with detailed timing, force-arrow evidence and strong friction-versus-inertia caveats.
scores.weakness: Mechanism 1's steady-motion friction-balancing step is ambitious for Year 7 first lesson and may be hard to execute cleanly.

scores.strength: Strong mechanistic diversity with a genuinely quantitative net-force subtraction mechanism and section-timing evidence, plus scientifically precise failure analysis distinguishing friction changes from inertia.
scores.weakness: Mechanism 3 packs many sub-activities (collision, loose-passenger, direction change) into 35 minutes, risking overload for one teacher managing 28 students.

scores.strength: Three distinct approaches with unusually careful conceptual guarding against the 'inertia force' misconception and explicit modelling limitations in the human model.
scores.weakness: The human-model evidence relies on students choosing how to move, which the candidate acknowledges but which weakens the phenomenon-to-evidence chain relative to the physical mechanisms.

scores.strength: Mechanism 2 (predict-observe-explain events) and the position-time strip model in Mechanism 3 give precise evidence and separate velocity from position well.
scores.weakness: Mechanism 1 (constant-force car) and the physical anchors inside M3 partly reuse comparative car pulling, slightly blurring the boundary between measurement and modelling approaches.

scores.strength: Distinct approaches (constant-force trials, rotating event circuit, opposing-force rig) with well-structured evidence tables and precise horizontal/vertical failure analysis.
scores.weakness: Mechanism 3 (opposing-force rig) shares the toy-car-with-spring-scales apparatus of Mechanism 1, so the distinctiveness rests mainly on the net-force framing rather than a wholly different core.

scores.strength: Three genuinely different cores (opposing-force measurement, predict-observe interruption sequence, balloon-thrust model) with detailed timing and strong friction-versus-inertia mitigations.
scores.weakness: The balloon-thrust tethered balanced-force step is conceptually subtle and may be difficult for a first-lesson Year 7 cohort to execute and interpret reliably.

scores.strength: Mechanism 1's vertical spring-scale motion profile is a genuinely novel, strongly quantitative phenomenon distinct from the event sequence and balloon model, with excellent evidence chains and scientifically rigorous failure analysis.
scores.weakness: The vertical motion-profile timing (5 cm per half-second by hand while reading a fluctuating scale) is demanding and the most execution-fragile part of the proposal.

major_errors: Mechanism 3 (force-map scenario cards) partly re-tests the same toy-car/spring-scale situations as Mechanism 1, and its physical phenomenon is thinner, edging toward a worksheet-with-arrows exercise.
scores.strength: Mechanism 2 (support-removal) is clean and Mechanism 1 gives good friction-versus-inertia evidence.
scores.weakness: Mechanism 3 leans on symbolic arrow-arranging rather than a strong independent physical phenomenon, and overlaps in apparatus with Mechanism 1.

scores.strength: Three distinct cores (abrupt-stop inertia, opposing-pull force balance, equal-pull mass comparison) with direct measurable evidence and clear force-arrow tables.
scores.weakness: Mechanisms 2 and 3 both use toy cars with spring scales and masses, so their distinctiveness rests on force-balance versus mass-comparison framing rather than fully different physical cores.

scores.strength: Clear three-way split (support removal, opposing-force cart, collision comparison) with numeric evidence tables and honest friction caveats in the collision mechanism.
scores.weakness: Mechanisms 2 and 3 both use toy cars in comparative measurement, so the second and third approaches are adjacent rather than sharply different in core method.

major_errors: Mechanisms 2 (opposed-force cart) and 3 (same-net-force mass comparison) share the same toy-car/spring-scale/lane apparatus and comparative-measurement core, weakening three-way distinctiveness.
scores.strength: The baseline-friction-subtraction step in Mechanism 3 is scientifically sophisticated and its failure mitigations are concrete.
scores.weakness: Two of the three mechanisms are variations on force-measured cart lanes, so genuine diversity is only partially achieved.

scores.strength: Three genuinely different cores (force-meter cart lanes, support-and-passenger sequence, balloon thrust-and-coast) with precise section-timing evidence and strong friction-baseline mitigations.
scores.weakness: Mechanism 1's steady-motion-force-then-add-0.3N procedure is subtle for a first lesson and depends on consistent manual pulling that the candidate acknowledges is fragile.

ranking.reason: Delivers the most distinct mechanistic diversity anchored by a genuinely novel quantitative vertical-force-profile mechanism, with the strongest evidence chains and most scientifically rigorous failure analysis.
Combines a truly quantitative net-force subtraction mechanism with distinct support-removal and collision approaches and precise, physics-aware failure reasoning.
Three clearly different cores including a balloon-thrust model, with measurable evidence and unusually thoughtful friction-baseline mitigations.
Strong three-way distinctiveness (measurement, event sequence, balloon model) with detailed timing and horizontal/vertical force analysis.
Clean measurement/support-removal/collision split with excellent timing detail and consistent friction-versus-inertia caveats.
Well-structured constant-force, event-circuit, and opposing-force mechanisms with precise evidence and horizontal/vertical failure guarding.
Three coherent cores with numeric evidence and honest friction caveats, though two rely on comparative car measurement.
Distinct approaches with exceptional misconception-guarding, slightly limited by the choice-dependent evidence in the human model.
Genuinely diverse trio with strong body-simulation failure analysis, held back by weaker human-model evidence.
Strong predict-observe and position-strip modelling, but partial reuse of comparative car pulling blurs approach boundaries.
Three usable cores with direct measurable evidence, though two share the car-plus-scale apparatus, narrowing distinctiveness.
Clear tow/support-removal/collision structure with good mitigations, but two mechanisms sit close in comparative-measurement approach.
Solid individual mechanisms and a sophisticated baseline-subtraction step, but two of three are variations on force-measured cart lanes.
Clear tables and mitigations, but the tug-test and constant-force pull share the same measurement core, weakening diversity.
Support-removal is clean, but Mechanism 3 overlaps apparatus with Mechanism 1 and leans on symbolic arrow work with a thinner physical phenomenon.

parsed.judge_observation: The field is uniformly competent and constraint-compliant; ranking is driven chiefly by genuine three-way mechanistic diversity and the presence of a strong quantitative phenomenon, with the weakest entries repeating the toy-car/spring-scale measurement core across two mechanisms.

measurement."},{"rank":8,"candidate":"T8-Full-14","reason":"Distinct approaches with exceptional misconception-guarding, slightly limited by the choice-dependent evidence in the human model."},{"rank":9,"candidate":"T8-Full-08","reason":"Genuinely diverse trio with strong body-simulation failure analysis, held back by weaker human-model evidence."},{"rank":10,"candidate":"T8-Full-07","reason":"Strong predict-observe and position-strip modelling, but partial reuse of comparative car pulling blurs approach boundaries."},{"rank":11,"candidate":"T8-Full-01","reason":"Three usable cores with direct measurable evidence, though two share the car-plus-scale apparatus, narrowing distinctiveness."},{"rank":12,"candidate":"T8-Full-11","reason":"Clear tow/support-removal/collision structure with good mitigations, but two mechanisms sit close in comparative-measurement approach."},{"rank":13,"candidate":"T8-Full-09","reason":"Solid individual mechanisms and a sophisticated baseline-subtraction step, but two of three are variations on force-measured cart lanes."},{"rank":14,"candidate":"T8-Full-06","reason":"Clear tables and mitigations, but the tug-test and constant-force pull share the same measurement core, weakening diversity."},{"rank":15,"candidate":"T8-Full-12","reason":"Support-removal is clean, but Mechanism 3 overlaps apparatus with Mechanism 1 and leans on symbolic arrow work with a thinner physical phenomenon."}],"judge_observation":"The field is uniformly competent and constraint-compliant; ranking is driven chiefly by genuine three-way mechanistic diversity and the presence of a strong quantitative phenomenon, with the weakest entries repeating the toy-car/spring-scale measurement core across two mechanisms."}

parsed.judge_observation: Fallback: candidates scored independently because the all-18 GLM prompt repeatedly emitted no final answer. Ranking derived from scores; ties broken by anonymised ID.

major_errors: Exceeds the 750-word maximum by 91 words.
scores.strength: The CEL tool, counterexample testing, revision cycle and individual precipitation question form a scientifically precise and highly usable bridge to A16.
scores.weakness: Its otherwise excellent response violates the explicit word limit and could be compressed without losing essential implementation detail.

major_errors: Exceeds the 750-word maximum by 107 words.
scores.strength: It combines a clear evidence-based decision strip, explicit O/I/L modelling, adversarial revision and a well-aligned individual dissolving check.
scores.weakness: Beyond the word-limit breach, the melting example slightly overstates how strongly a reported state change alone establishes that no new substance formed.

major_errors: Exceeds the 750-word maximum by 155 words.
scores.strength: The evidence ladder coherently integrates observations, alternative explanations, confidence, revision and direct transfer into the A16 practical.
scores.weakness: The response is substantially over length, and the card instruction to record two observations may require more precise case-writing than is specified.

major_errors: Exceeds the 750-word maximum by 160 words.
scores.strength: The student-designed evidence mat is tested, challenged and visibly revised while preserving uncertainty and strong conceptual extension.
scores.weakness: The individual prompt does not explicitly request an inference even though separating inference from observation is awarded a marking point, and the response is substantially over length.

scores.strength: This is a concise, fully compliant and classroom-ready lesson whose student-built mat, challenge cycle, dual-case assessment and A16 handoff align exceptionally well.
scores.weakness: The four-point marking scheme compresses several distinct demands from the two-part check into broad points, slightly limiting diagnostic granularity.

scores.strength: It meets the output contract while providing precise science, a genuinely revised reasoning artifact, rich accessibility mechanisms and sophisticated uncertainty-focused extension.
scores.weakness: The final seven minutes may be tight if every student is expected to state an observation, inference and limit orally in a class of approximately 30.

major_errors: Exceeds the 750-word maximum by 183 words.
scores.strength: Counterexample cards genuinely stress-test the student-built tool, and the modelling and assessment consistently distinguish observations, inferences and evidential limits.
scores.weakness: The response is considerably over length and includes more scenario and operational detail than is necessary for implementation.

major_errors: Exceeds the 750-word maximum by 138 words.
scores.strength: It offers a coherent adversarial build-test-revise sequence, strong trap cases and a diagnostic individual question addressing dissolving, temperature and reversibility misconceptions.
scores.weakness: It breaches the word limit, and describing the starting material as a decision-tool template makes the artifact marginally less student-authored than the strongest alternatives.

major_errors: Exceeds the 750-word maximum by 97 words.
scores.strength: The contrasting supplied-record model operationalises observation and inference especially well, while the revised map and A16 transfer are clear and usable.
scores.weakness: The response exceeds the word maximum, and the rust-like exam case depends on a best-supported chemical inference rather than especially discriminating product evidence.

major_errors: Exceeds the 750-word maximum by 95 words.
scores.strength: The decision map has all essential scientific guardrails and is repeatedly tested, debated, revised and transferred to A16.
scores.weakness: The access section assigns three rotating roles to pairs without clarifying how this will operate, creating a minor feasibility inconsistency in addition to the word-limit breach.

major_errors: Exceeds the 750-word maximum by 51 words.
scores.strength: It provides exceptionally clear observation-versus-inference modelling, a well-designed confidence-aware pathway and a strong individual dissolving assessment.
scores.weakness: Its only substantial compliance defect is exceeding the maximum length, though the excess is smaller than in most noncompliant candidates.

major_errors: Exceeds the 750-word maximum by 19 words.
scores.strength: This is a dense, highly implementable lesson with authentic student authorship, adversarial tool testing, excellent marking points and a precise A16 routine.
scores.weakness: It narrowly but explicitly violates the 750-word maximum.

major_errors: Exceeds the 750-word maximum by 269 words.
scores.strength: Scientifically and pedagogically, it is comprehensive, with strong modelling, a genuine stress-tested artifact, diagnostic assessment and concrete attention supports.
scores.weakness: At 1019 words it exceeds the maximum by more than one third, materially undermining the required precision and efficiency despite the quality of its content.

major_errors: Exceeds the 750-word maximum by 153 words.
scores.strength: The evidence ladder, explicit calibration of misconceptions and qualified precipitate assessment create a coherent preparation sequence for A16.
scores.weakness: The response is substantially over length, and five minutes is ambitious for groups to construct the first A3 ladder before beginning case testing.

major_errors: Exceeds the 750-word maximum by 94 words.
scores.strength: It accurately centres new-substance evidence, includes a usable revised route and gives a cautious individual evaluation of suggestive chemical-change observations.
scores.weakness: The timed sequence ends with assessment rather than reserving time for the otherwise explicit A16 handoff, and the prompt does not directly request a named limitation.

major_errors: Exceeds the 750-word maximum by 170 words.
scores.strength: The movable Change Claim Board is genuinely constructed and revised, and the A16 rehearsal makes the practical transfer unusually concrete.
scores.weakness: The response is substantially over length, and its A16 recording plan mentions odour without immediately attaching the later safety qualification against deliberate smelling.

major_errors: Exceeds the 750-word maximum by 90 words.
scores.strength: The personal evidence-to-claim card, ambiguous stress test and two-part individual check provide a strong progression from construction to independent reasoning.
scores.weakness: It exceeds the word limit, and its sequence has less explicit whole-class scientific calibration after revision than the highest-ranked lessons.

major_errors: Exceeds the 750-word maximum by 11 words.
scores.strength: This is an exceptionally dense and rigorous protocol lesson with deterministic contrast cases, genuine model revision, excellent misconception diagnosis and a direct A16 handoff.
scores.weakness: It misses the word maximum by only 11 words, but that remains an explicit output-contract violation.

ranking.reason: It best combines full compliance, scientific precision, feasible construction and revision, diagnostic individual assessment and an operational A16 transfer.
It is fully compliant and conceptually deep, with especially strong uncertainty reasoning and ADHD-supportive access, but has a slightly tighter final consolidation.
Its highly efficient evidence protocol and deterministic individual check are outstanding, with only an 11-word contract breach preventing a higher rank.
It delivers excellent student authorship, adversarial testing and practical transfer but exceeds the maximum by 19 words.
Its modelling, artifact and assessment are exceptionally precise and feasible, though it is 51 words over the limit.
The sophisticated CEL structure and explicit limitations make it highly usable, but its 91-word excess is a clear compliance loss.
It offers one of the strongest observation-inference comparisons and a clear A16-ready map, offset by overlength and a somewhat inferential rust assessment.
Its compact personal tool and two-part independent check are excellent, though it is over length and has less post-revision calibration.
The O/I/L method and dissolving check are very strong, with a minor epistemic imprecision in the melting model and a substantial word-limit breach.
Trap cases and adversarial testing produce strong diagnosis and revision, but the artifact is somewhat template-led and the response is over length.
It is scientifically comprehensive and instructionally coherent, but its 155-word excess reduces compliance and efficiency.
The lesson has a sound evidence ladder and assessment but is substantially over length and allocates little initial construction time.
Its reasoning map and extension are strong, though the pair-role mismatch and word-limit violation reduce feasibility and compliance.
It provides excellent counterexample-driven stress testing but is considerably longer than allowed and less efficient than higher-ranked equivalents.
The scientific core and artifact are strong, but the sequence does not reserve time for its A16 handoff and the check only implicitly elicits a limitation.
Its A16 rehearsal and movable model are valuable, but substantial overlength and the loosely qualified odour reference lower its standing.
The lesson is conceptually strong, but its assessment prompt-marking mismatch and 160-word excess make it less precise than nearby candidates.
Despite excellent content, exceeding the maximum by 269 words is the largest contract and efficiency failure in the set.

parsed.judge_observation: All 18 candidates meet the four cap-triggering requirements, so no critical cap applies, but only T2-Full-05 and T2-Full-15 comply with the 750-word maximum.

scores.strength: Three fundamentally distinct physical phenomena (controlled force-time intervals, friction-time support removal, contact collision) each yield precise measurable tables and sketches that map directly onto inertia and net force.
scores.weakness: Marble release variability still leaves the collision mass comparison more qualitative than the timed car trials.

scores.strength: Vertical spring-scale motion profile uniquely measures force excess or deficit during start/steady/stop phases, cleanly separating constant velocity from acceleration with recordable scale data.
scores.weakness: Pendulum follow-through comparison introduces minor uncontrolled swing variables that dilute pure inertia evidence.

scores.strength: Clean triad of spring-scale car timing, support-removal, and dual-scale net-force tug each supplies tables of force readings versus observed motion change.
scores.weakness: Car friction scatter requires repeated trials and explicit teacher framing to avoid misattribution.

scores.strength: Card-strip dual rest-and-motion trials, quantitative net-force subtraction, and loose-passenger collision together cover all three First-Law facets with concrete displacement data.
scores.weakness: Shared collision lanes slightly compress management bandwidth for one teacher with 28 students.

scores.strength: Force-meter steady-versus-excess pulls, multi-trial support-plus-passenger sequence, and balloon thrust-coast phases produce clear before-during-after force-motion records.
scores.weakness: Balloon inflation consistency remains residual despite width gauges and repeated trials.

major_errors: Mechanism 3 human model uses largely symbolic string lengths rather than independently measured physical forces
scores.strength: Inertia card-pull and dual-scale equal/unequal car trials supply rigorous tables and force diagrams; failure analysis correctly flags model limits.
scores.weakness: Human-motion model evidence is consistency of enacted diagrams rather than a passive physical phenomenon.

scores.strength: Opposing-force cart, multi-interruption inertia sequence and balloon-thrust model each link observable motion phases to balanced versus unbalanced conditions.
scores.weakness: Balloon thrust scatter and the three-part interruption sequence risk incomplete data collection inside 35 minutes.

major_errors: Human body-pull simulation can be misread as requiring continuous force agents
scores.strength: Card-cup mass flick, spring-scale car loading and structured body-role scenarios cover rest, motion change and mass-inertia with explicit tables.
scores.weakness: Body simulation evidence is partly role-play rather than a pure external physical interaction.

scores.strength: Tow trials, sudden support removal and controlled collisions each generate force-arrow sketches and mass-dependent distance or time data.
scores.weakness: Release-speed inconsistency in collisions and occasional equipment sharing reduce measurement purity.

scores.strength: Classic support-removal, dual-scale balance and marble-car collision triad each yield simple, recordable yes/no and distance tables.
scores.weakness: Evidence remains largely qualitative; mass-inertia comparison lacks timed force control.

major_errors: Human position-strip model is rule-driven representation rather than measured physical response
scores.strength: Constant-force car comparison plus multi-event POE sequence produce clear force-versus-motion tables and sketches.
scores.weakness: Abstract cup-position model and multi-event packing stretch both evidence quality and 35-minute feasibility.

major_errors: Circuit rotation of three event types increases management load for one teacher
scores.strength: Constant-force car, multi-station inertia circuit and dual-scale rig supply prediction-observation tables linked to net force.
scores.weakness: Station rotation and multi-copy preparation make one-teacher control of 28 students borderline.

major_errors: Mechanisms 2 and 3 both rely on similar cart-scale apparatus, reducing genuine diversity
scores.strength: Card-removal, opposed-force and baseline-adjusted mass comparison each include explicit force and displacement records.
scores.weakness: Two-wave station sharing and residual friction baseline complexity compress the 35-minute window.

major_errors: Mechanisms 1 and 3 both centre on car-based sudden change, limiting approach diversity
scores.strength: Abrupt-stop card/car pair, four-station force-balance and equal-pull mass comparison generate before-after sketches and distance tables.
scores.weakness: Station rotation plus overlapping car themes leave less clean separation of mechanistic types.

major_errors: Physical actions and evidence tables are less tightly sequenced and sometimes qualitative only
scores.strength: Cart coast/pull, support-removal stations and scenario-card force-arrow tests still address balanced versus unbalanced motion.
scores.weakness: Force-map circuit risks becoming a worksheet exercise; evidence chain is weaker than timed or measured competitors.

ranking.reason: Highest combined specificity of three distinct physical phenomena, measurable evidence chains and realistic failure mitigations.
Unique vertical force-profile mechanism plus strong POE and balloon options deliver maximum conceptual and evidential diversity.
Textbook-clean triad of measurement, support removal and dual-scale balance with consistently high evidence quality.
Excellent net-force arithmetic and passenger-inertia coverage with only minor lane-sharing feasibility cost.
Solid force-meter, multi-trial inertia and balloon-coast sequence with thorough failure analysis.
Strong first two mechanisms and honest model caveats, offset by partly symbolic third mechanism.
Good opposing-force, interruption and balloon variety but balloon scatter and sequence packing reduce reliability.
Clear three approaches yet body-simulation evidence is weaker than pure physical phenomena.
Competent tow-support-collision set with solid tables, limited by release consistency and sharing.
Reliable classic trio but less innovative and more qualitative than higher-ranked peers.
Useful car and POE cores undermined by abstract position-strip model and multi-event crowding.
Sound scientific content but circuit rotation and multi-station logistics lower one-teacher feasibility.
Mechanically sound yet two cart-scale mechanisms reduce genuine diversity and station waves add friction.
Overlapping car themes and station rotation produce weaker diversity and management scores.
Least specific action sequences and weakest measurable evidence chains among the fifteen.

parsed.judge_observation: Highest ranks reward three mechanistically distinct physical phenomena with tight evidence-to-principle chains and realistic failure analysis; lowest ranks show overlapping approaches or thinner recordable data.

scores.strength: Provides a complete, well-aligned architecture with precise criteria, checkpoint controls, absence routes, authenticity checks, moderation, and all required blocking conditions.
scores.weakness: The authenticity section conditionally permits absent students to use expressly authorised group data despite the stricter supplied requirement to use secondary data, and it adds a non-mandated alternative-task block.

scores.strength: Covers every required section clearly and accurately, including independent two-teacher moderation and a fully specified individual-only absence alternative.
scores.weakness: Its practical-absence row briefly suggests that uncollected group data could be teacher-authorised, which is less strict than the requirement to use supplied secondary data.

scores.strength: Delivers precise criterion descriptors and especially strong treatment of data provenance, checkpoint records, remoderation, and final decision-making.
scores.weakness: It adds a separate overall block for falsely claimed practical participation, although this largely overlaps the required authenticity and unauthorised-data block.

scores.strength: Offers an exceptionally explicit and internally coherent architecture, including band-to-mark mappings, criterion-specific checkpoint caps, an exact 24-portfolio moderation minimum, and all hidden-key requirements.
scores.weakness: The final-submission row could state the direct consequence of a wholly missing final component more explicitly rather than relying on later blocking provisions.

scores.strength: Provides a highly operational design with explicit numerical checkpoint caps, logged secondary-data authorisation, a complete alternative-task structure, and decisive moderation procedures.
scores.weakness: Capping every individual-analysis criterion after a missing outline or draft is broader than necessary and could have been mapped more narrowly to corresponding evidence.

scores.strength: Meets all substantive rubric and hidden-key requirements with excellent data-provenance descriptors, exact moderation arithmetic, and a clear replacement task.
scores.weakness: At 1569 words, it violates the explicit requirement to keep the architecture under 1500 words.

scores.strength: Contains comprehensive criteria, detailed authenticity records, a robust moderation sequence, and every required blocking and extended-absence rule.
scores.weakness: It exceeds the word limit by 87 words and allows an adjusted checkpoint to count as compliant, weakening the absolute rule that a draft missing at the specified checkpoint cannot receive full marks.

scores.strength: Substantively satisfies every required architecture element and gives particularly rigorous contribution verification, secondary-data logging, and systemic remoderation steps.
scores.weakness: It exceeds the 1500-word limit by 65 words and introduces an unnecessarily elaborate third-marker process.

scores.strength: Accurately supplies all criteria, allocations, absence pathways, authenticity controls, moderation requirements, and mandatory blocking decisions in a concise structure.
scores.weakness: The checkpoint mapping of the Week 1 outline only to A2 and the Week 2 draft only to A1 is somewhat counterintuitive and could be justified more clearly.

scores.strength: Presents a polished and complete architecture with explicit band ranges, fixed checkpoint caps, exact absence routing, and a well-defined moderation lead.
scores.weakness: The selected checkpoint-to-analysis-criterion mapping is defensible but less transparent than directly linking each draft to every criterion evidenced by it.

scores.strength: Fully operationalises the supplied rules through clear criterion descriptors, exact draft caps, strict secondary-data pathways, cohort-wide consistency review, and complete blocking logic.
scores.weakness: The added indicative overall-band thresholds are not required and slightly increase complexity without improving compliance.

scores.strength: Provides an exact 12/12/6 design with strong checkpoint enforcement, detailed provenance controls, a complete individual alternative, and systematic moderation review.
scores.weakness: Final moderation decisions are assigned jointly to the two markers without naming a tie-break decision-maker if they remain unable to agree.

scores.strength: Includes all mandatory criteria, mark checks, absence categories, authenticity mechanisms, moderation thresholds, and hidden-key blocking conditions.
scores.weakness: It improperly allows an authorised absence adjustment to remove a missing-draft cap and adds unsupported overall blocks for contribution-statement and checkpoint deficiencies.

scores.strength: Meets all core allocation, descriptor, absence, authenticity, moderation, and required blocking provisions in a clear document under the word limit.
scores.weakness: It incorrectly elevates any missing checkpoint evidence into an overall Proficient/Exemplary block rather than limiting only the corresponding criterion's maximum mark.

scores.strength: Offers a complete and readable architecture with strong four-band descriptors, explicit data-pack authorisation, exact moderation numbers, and all mandatory blocks.
scores.weakness: It adds an unsupported overall block when contribution documentation is absent or inaccurate, even though the supplied blocking rule is specifically tied to the individual analysis.

scores.strength: Nearly exhaustively satisfies the task with clear performance ranges, exact checkpoint effects, a properly weighted individual alternative, and a robust lead-teacher moderation process.
scores.weakness: The authenticity protocol allows expressly authorised use of missed group data, creating tension with the stricter practical-absence requirement to use supplied secondary data instead.

scores.strength: Combines precise criterion-level caps, strict absence and provenance rules, complete authenticity handling, and an exact two-teacher moderation process in a highly coherent architecture.
scores.weakness: Its added overall numerical band thresholds are unnecessary and are not derived from the supplied requirements.

scores.strength: Satisfies every rubric and hidden-key requirement precisely while remaining concise, internally consistent, operational, and comfortably within the word limit.
scores.weakness: The 8-mark G2 and A2 criteria combine several skills, making them somewhat less diagnostically granular than a design using more smaller criteria.

ranking.reason: It is the most concise fully compliant response, with exact checkpoint caps, strict data pathways, complete moderation, and no unsupported overall blocks.
It is exceptionally explicit and accurate, including criterion-specific consequences and exact moderation arithmetic, with only a minor final-submission wording gap.
It provides an almost faultless operational architecture with particularly clear provenance, checkpoint, and blocking controls.
It covers every requirement precisely and adds strong cohort-wide consistency review without introducing substantive conflicts.
Its explicit caps, authorisation log, alternative-task construction, and moderation leadership make it highly implementable.
It is highly complete and precise, with only a largely overlapping additional authenticity block reducing exactness.
It is fully compliant and well structured, though its checkpoint-to-analysis mapping is slightly less intuitive than the higher-ranked designs.
It satisfies all core rules concisely but uses a somewhat unusual division of the analysis criteria between checkpoints.
It is substantively complete and rigorous, but its joint final-decision arrangement lacks a clear tie-break authority.
It is comprehensive and accurate apart from a slight ambiguity about authorising group data for a student absent from collection.
It is strongly aligned overall, but its group-data authorisation wording and additional alternative-task block reduce strict fidelity.
It meets virtually all requirements but contains the same minor tension between the strict absence pathway and later group-data authorisation wording.
It is complete and polished, but adds a contribution-documentation overall block not required by the supplied blocking rule.
Its substantive architecture is excellent, but the response fails the explicit 1500-word limit.
It meets the substantive rubric but exceeds the word limit and adds avoidable moderation complexity.
It covers every required core element but incorrectly converts missing checkpoint evidence into an additional overall classification block.
Despite excellent substantive coverage, it both exceeds the word limit and weakens the fixed checkpoint consequence through replacement deadlines.
It includes all hidden-key facts but most clearly departs from the supplied architecture by permitting checkpoint-cap exceptions and imposing two unsupported overall blocks.

parsed.judge_observation: All 18 candidates satisfy the three hidden-key requirements, so no critical or major cap was triggered; differentiation rests mainly on strict checkpoint fidelity, unsupported extra blocks, data-pathway consistency, and compliance with the 1500-word limit.
